task: R6-B — Healthcare practice state privacy add-on (Massachusetts Ch. 93H + 201 CMR 17 context)
disclaimer: Operational compliance research — not legal advice. MA data-security regulations (201 CMR 17.00) apply to persons owning/licensing personal information; confirm applicability to PHI held by medical practices.
policy: Public .gov sources only; mass.gov regulation portal 403 at harvest — statute primary.

data_security_breach.label: M.G.L. c. 93H — Security Breaches & Safeguard Regulations

cited_facts.text: Breach of security means unauthorized acquisition or use of unencrypted personal information (or encrypted data with compromised key) creating substantial risk of identity theft or fraud against a Massachusetts resident.

DCABR shall adopt regulations requiring any person that owns or licenses personal information about a Massachusetts resident to safeguard it — consistent with applicable federal regulations by which the person is regulated (including HIPAA-covered entities).
REQUIRES_CONFIRMATION — 201 CMR 17.00 implementing regulations (mass.gov) returned HTTP 403 at harvest; safeguard standards referenced by §2(a) require separate regulation harvest.

citation.source: 201 CMR 17.00 — Standards for Protection of Personal Information

gap.message: 201 CMR 17.00 regulation text ACCESS_RESTRICTED (mass.gov 403); statute framework FOUND via c. 93H.
